title: The One with the Inappropriate Sister

jokes.text: Top shelf, expired products
jokes.setup: Ross is reorganizing the fridge with meats/dairy on bottom, fruits/vegetables in middle
jokes.punchline: The top shelf is specifically for expired products - treating spoiled food as a category to organize rather than throw away
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Ross's obsessive organization taken to an absurd extreme. The escalating organizational categories build to an unexpected punchline.

jokes.text: Dude, you just described seven days' worth of stuff. Spread it out a little.
jokes.setup: Ross lists his productive morning activities (bank, post office, dry cleaners)
jokes.punchline: Joey points out Ross accomplished a week's worth of errands in one morning, highlighting his manic productivity
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Ross's type-A personality and Joey's laid-back approach. Solid observational humor about productivity anxiety.

jokes.text: I'm not unemployed. I'm on sabbatical. / Hey, don't get religious on me, okay?
jokes.setup: Ross corrects Joey that he's on sabbatical, not unemployed
jokes.punchline: Joey misunderstands 'sabbatical' as religious terminology, responding with 'don't get religious on me'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey malapropism showing his misunderstanding of academic terminology. The religious confusion is perfectly in character and economically delivered.

jokes.text: Hello, Chandler Bing. / Hello, Mr. Bing. / I love you.
jokes.setup: Chandler answers the phone normally expecting a business call
jokes.punchline: The caller escalates from formal greeting to creepy 'I love you' declaration
jokes.explanation: Sets up the stalker scenario with good escalation from normal to creepy. The formal-to-intimate shift creates discomfort humor.

jokes.text: It's been 6 months! It's not funny!
jokes.setup: Chandler is being harassed by mysterious caller
jokes.punchline: Reveals this has been going on for 6 months, showing the extent of his torment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the duration of harassment. Creates sympathy and cringe humor as Chandler's frustration peaks.

jokes.text: I can see you, Mr. Bing. You look sexy in your new suit.
jokes.setup: The harassment phone calls continue
jokes.punchline: The caller reveals they can physically see Chandler, making it more threatening and specific
jokes.explanation: Escalates the stalker scenario from phone calls to physical surveillance. The 'sexy in your new suit' detail adds creepy specificity.

jokes.text: And that's Wednesday.
jokes.setup: Chandler has just had a disturbing phone harassment incident
jokes.punchline: Joey casually summarizes this traumatic experience as just 'Wednesday' - routine entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan punchline that reframes Chandler's trauma as casual entertainment. Excellent economy and timing, showing Joey's oblivious perspective.

jokes.text: The British are coming?
jokes.setup: Phoebe enthusiastically announces 'Guess what?'
jokes.punchline: Chandler responds with Revolutionary War reference instead of engaging with her actual excitement
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deflection with historical reference. Good misdirection from Phoebe's earnest setup to his sarcastic non-sequitur.

jokes.text: I'll be spreading joy to the people. Last year I spread a little joy, but not enough. So this year I'm doing the whole city.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains her Christmas charity work with bell ringing
jokes.punchline: Her earnest goal to 'spread joy to the whole city' shows her naive optimism about her individual impact
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe character moment showing her grandiose but well-meaning plans. The escalation from 'a little joy' to 'the whole city' is endearing.

jokes.text: I knew a girl in high school who did that. She was very popular.
jokes.setup: Phoebe innocently says she's 'doing the whole city'
jokes.punchline: Joey makes a sexual double entendre about a promiscuous girl from high school
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey innuendo taking Phoebe's innocent statement in a sexual direction. Predictable but in character and well-timed.

jokes.text: It's weird, you're starting to sound a lot like Chandler lately.
jokes.setup: Joey just made a sarcastic innuendo comment
jokes.punchline: Ross observes that Joey is picking up Chandler's sarcastic speaking patterns
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-observation about character influence. Sets up the idea that the friends are rubbing off on each other.

jokes.text: That is so not true.
jokes.setup: Ross says Joey is starting to sound like Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joey denies it using a distinctly Chandler-esque speech pattern ('That is so not')
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic punchline where Joey proves Ross's point while denying it. The specific phrasing 'so not' is distinctly Chandler's style, making the denial self-defeating.

jokes.text: They never give a good spot to a rookie... but I'm the only one who can say 'Merry Christmas' in 25 languages. I lied!
jokes.setup: Phoebe boasts about her unique qualification for getting a prime bell-ringing spot
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits she lied about speaking 25 languages
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment with great misdirection. She builds up her accomplishment then immediately deflates it with brutal honesty. The timing of the admission is perfect.

jokes.text: See? Still pretending he's not interested.
jokes.setup: Danny walks by without acknowledging them
jokes.punchline: Monica interprets his complete lack of interaction as 'pretending not to be interested'
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's tendency to over-interpret social situations. Her delusion about Danny's disinterest being an act is classic Monica meddling setup.

jokes.text: He's coming over. Pretend we've forgotten who he is. / Hey, guys. / Hey, Danny.
jokes.setup: Monica instructs them to pretend they forgot Danny's name as strategy
jokes.punchline: They immediately greet him by name, failing at the simple deception within seconds
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom timing failure joke. The immediate contradiction of Monica's instruction creates good physical comedy through incompetence.

jokes.text: On the date, I'll be able to talk to her directly?
jokes.setup: Monica has been aggressively arranging Danny and Rachel's date by speaking for Rachel
jokes.punchline: Danny's polite question about whether he'll actually be able to communicate with Rachel herself
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation about Monica's overbearing matchmaking. Danny's dry delivery highlights how absurd Monica's intermediary role has become.

jokes.text: Well, Talia Shire suddenly became available. / She's a woman! / What can I say? She nailed it!
jokes.setup: Joey asks how he didn't get a part written for a 29-year-old Italian actor from Queens
jokes.punchline: The role went to Talia Shire, a woman, because she 'nailed it' despite being wrong for every specification
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Hollywood logic where talent trumps any demographic requirements. The agent's casual 'she nailed it' response to Joey's legitimate confusion is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You'll just say no again, but... gay porn?
jokes.setup: Joey asks if there's anything else available after losing the perfect role
jokes.punchline: Agent reluctantly offers gay porn, knowing it will be rejected but having nothing else
jokes.explanation: Standard 90s edgy humor about desperate actor situations. The agent's resigned delivery sells the desperation but it's not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Is that a new suede jacket? It looks really expensive. / Yeah. I guess.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is collecting charity donations for the poor
jokes.punchline: She guilt-trips a donor about their expensive jacket before accepting their money
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Phoebe's lack of tact while charity collecting. Her direct approach to wealth inequality creates uncomfortable but funny moments.

jokes.text: Just get your nails done? / Yes, Phoebe, but this is all I have, okay?
jokes.setup: Phoebe continues interrogating the wealthy donor
jokes.punchline: The donor gets defensive, revealing they're being guilt-tripped despite only having a small amount to give
jokes.explanation: Continues the cringe scenario with escalating defensiveness. The donor's frustrated explanation adds realism to Phoebe's tactless charity work.

jokes.text: You can't take money out. / I'm making change for the bus. / Can't you leave the dollar for the poor? / I'm poor. I gotta take the bus. / Bite me, blondie!
jokes.setup: Someone puts money in then tries to take change out
jokes.punchline: Escalating argument where the person reveals they're poor too and need bus fare, ending with 'Bite me, blondie!'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the absurdity of charity collection. The donor's legitimate need for bus fare versus Phoebe's charity creates real conflict with a harsh punchline.

jokes.text: What are the chances I could get those guys to star in it? / Wait a second. I could star in it!
jokes.setup: Joey considers writing something and getting Good Will Hunting guys to star
jokes.punchline: He suddenly realizes he himself could be the star, as if this obvious option never occurred to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic where he overlooks the obvious solution. The delayed realization that he could star in his own project shows his endearing self-absorption and confusion.

jokes.text: I can't write. I mean, I'm an actor. I could act like a writer.
jokes.setup: Joey realizes he needs to write but doesn't know how
jokes.punchline: His solution is to 'act like a writer' - applying his acting skills to writing itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic applying his only skill (acting) to an unrelated problem. The idea of 'acting like a writer' instead of actually writing is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Done. / And it can't be Joey. / It's not. / Or Joseph.
jokes.setup: Ross gives Joey the simple task of naming his main character
jokes.punchline: Ross has to preemptively ban Joey's own name and its variants, implying Joey was going to use them
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Joey's narcissism and lack of imagination. Ross's preemptive bans suggest this is predictable Joey behavior, and the quick back-and-forth timing is excellent.

jokes.text: You meddled in our relationship! / You had no relationship!
jokes.setup: Rachel blames Monica for interfering with her and Danny
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt observation that there was no actual relationship to meddle with
jokes.explanation: Good reality check from Monica puncturing Rachel's delusion. The stark honesty contrasts well with Rachel's dramatic accusation.

jokes.text: She was kind of stupid.
jokes.setup: Monica tries to reassure Rachel about the girl Danny was with
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately agrees and disparages the other woman despite never meeting her
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's petty side coming out when she feels threatened. The quick agreement to disparage a stranger reveals her insecurity.

jokes.text: Chandler Bing, seven! Chandler Bing, zero!
jokes.setup: Chandler and Joey are playing some kind of basketball game
jokes.punchline: Chandler announces the score like a sports broadcaster, referring to himself in third person
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with Chandler's self-aggrandizing sports commentary. The third-person announcement of his own victory is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Well, you suck. But at least you suck at a man's game now.
jokes.setup: Joey apparently missed his shot badly
jokes.punchline: Chandler's backhanded encouragement that failure at sports is still more masculine than other activities
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with toxic masculinity undertones typical of 90s comedy. The backhanded compliment structure works well for his character.

jokes.text: We said goodbye to that when we invented hammer darts.
jokes.setup: Joey wants their security deposit back
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals they previously invented 'hammer darts' implying extensive apartment damage
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing past destructive games. 'Hammer darts' is an inherently funny and dangerous-sounding concept that explains their cavalier attitude toward apartment damage.

jokes.text: Do you even remember which part of the wall is not spackle?
jokes.setup: They're discussing apartment damage from their games
jokes.punchline: Joey implies so much wall damage that the original wall is now a minority of the surface
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the damage theme. The image of walls being more spackle than original material is absurdist and creates a strong visual.

jokes.text: No, that's trash, young lady. You can't— Stop that young lady! She donated trash!
jokes.setup: Phoebe is managing her charity collection bucket
jokes.punchline: Someone donates actual garbage and Phoebe has to chase them down, treating littering as charity fraud
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing the reality of charity collection. Phoebe's outraged pursuit of the trash-donator creates escalating absurdity.

jokes.text: The charity's on fire! Help! Thank you. I need that!
jokes.setup: Chandler and Joey's fireball game has apparently caused actual fire
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treats the fire emergency as an opportunity to collect more donations
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation where disaster becomes opportunity. Phoebe's single-minded focus on charity collection even during emergencies is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: A room. A man enters. He looks suspicious.
jokes.setup: Joey is supposed to have written five pages of script
jokes.punchline: His entire output is three basic, uninspired sentences describing obvious visual elements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey writing showing his lack of imagination and understanding of narrative. The barebones, clichéd description reveals his amateur approach to creative writing.

jokes.text: That's the game we played. It's great. You take a tennis ball, a bowl and lighter—
jokes.setup: Ross asks about the 'Official Rulebook of Fireball' instead of Joey's script
jokes.punchline: Joey enthusiastically explains the dangerous game involving fire and tennis balls
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's misplaced priorities and enthusiasm for dangerous games over actual work. The interrupted explanation builds anticipation for the chaos.

jokes.text: Not the creator of 'Crazy Lawsuit Game.'
jokes.setup: Ross is frustrated that Joey spent time on game rules instead of writing
jokes.punchline: He renames Fireball as 'Crazy Lawsuit Game' highlighting its dangerous liability
jokes.explanation: Excellent renaming joke that recontextualizes their 'fun' game as a legal nightmare. Ross's practical perspective contrasts perfectly with Joey and Chandler's recklessness.

jokes.text: I thought I heard you. / Hey, great, you're up. / Rachel, my sister Krista. / Nice to meet you. / I wish you'd warned me. I'd have fixed myself up. / Like it would help. / You are so bad. / You are. / You are. / You are! / You are so dead. / I'm gonna get you. Come here!
jokes.setup: Rachel meets Danny's sister after seeing them together on the subway
jokes.punchline: The siblings engage in increasingly flirtatious banter and physical play that seems inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy setup where inappropriate sibling behavior makes everyone uncomfortable. The escalating flirtation creates sustained awkwardness that drives the episode's central conflict.

jokes.text: Does this look like a garbage can to you? Does it look like an ashtray? Does it look like a urinal?
jokes.setup: Phoebe complains about inappropriate donations to her charity bucket
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of disgusting things people have used her charity bucket for, ending with urinal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate public behavior. The progression from garbage to urinal shows increasing violation of social norms and Phoebe's frustration.

jokes.text: No more Mrs. Nice Bucket.
jokes.setup: Phoebe decides to get tougher with people misusing her charity collection
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'Mrs. Nice Guy' but substitutes 'Bucket' referencing her charity collection bucket
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay adapting a common phrase to Phoebe's specific situation. The substitution of 'Bucket' for 'Guy' is unexpected and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: But not totally back. Street Phoebe wouldn't be friends with you guys.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she'll go back to being 'Street Phoebe' to handle tough situations
jokes.punchline: She clarifies she won't go completely back because her street persona wouldn't associate with her current friends
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight showing Phoebe's self-awareness about her past versus present identity. The honesty about class differences and friendship is both funny and touching.

jokes.text: Well, you weighed 200 pounds.
jokes.setup: Monica claims she was undefeated at wrestling
jokes.punchline: Ross explains her success was due to significant weight advantage, not skill
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Monica's childhood weight problems. Ross's matter-of-fact deflation of her wrestling pride is typical sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: Still, I was quick as a cat.
jokes.setup: Ross points out Monica won wrestling due to her weight
jokes.punchline: Monica claims she was also quick, which contrasts ironically with being 200 pounds
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's inability to accept criticism of her abilities. The 'quick as a cat' claim is endearingly delusional given the weight context.

jokes.text: Not since I got too strong for you.
jokes.setup: Ross says they don't wrestle anymore
jokes.punchline: Monica claims it's because she became too strong for him, challenging his masculinity
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature driving her to claim physical superiority over Ross. Sets up their wrestling match by challenging his ego.

jokes.text: Oh, fine. Ready? Wrestle!
jokes.setup: Monica and Ross argue about who's stronger
jokes.punchline: They immediately engage in actual wrestling, with Ross's dramatic 'Wrestle!' announcement
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff of their argument. Ross's formal 'Wrestle!' announcement treats it like a sport rather than sibling squabbling.

jokes.text: Okay, you know what? Actually, that helps a lot. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Rachel watches Ross and Monica wrestle after asking about normal sibling behavior
jokes.punchline: She's satisfied that their normal wrestling proves Danny and his sister's behavior is inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good payoff using Ross and Monica's normal sibling dynamic to highlight how abnormal Danny's behavior was. Rachel's relief is palpable and relatable.

jokes.text: Yesterday's pages did not reflect my best work.
jokes.setup: Ross says Joey needs to redo yesterday's pages
jokes.punchline: Joey uses pretentious artistic language to describe his obviously terrible writing
jokes.explanation: Joey applying artistic pretension to his objectively terrible three-sentence script. The disconnect between his self-perception and reality is perfectly Joey.

jokes.text: If you were a friend, you'd be doing the same. / So being a friend means being a jerk? / If it does, then you're an amazing friend of mine.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler argue about what friendship means regarding Joey
jokes.punchline: Ross's backhanded compliment calling Chandler an 'amazing friend' because he's being a jerk
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation where Ross turns Chandler's insult into a backhanded compliment. The logic is twisted but follows from the setup perfectly.

jokes.text: You said you would, but you didn't. / Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Chandler accuses Ross of not paying for tickets he destroyed
jokes.punchline: Ross casually admits Chandler is right, deflating the argument completely
jokes.explanation: Good deflation where Ross's casual admission undercuts the dramatic argument. His absent-minded 'Oh, yeah' shows he forgot his promise entirely.

jokes.text: Chasing the churro guy is not jogging.
jokes.setup: Danny claims they were 'jogging' when they saw a bird
jokes.punchline: Krista reveals they were actually chasing a food vendor, not exercising
jokes.explanation: Good reality check revealing Danny's lie about healthy exercise. The image of chasing a churro vendor is inherently funny and very New York.

jokes.text: Damn. I got it on my pants. / Here, I'll get it. / We better take them off or the stain will set.
jokes.setup: Danny spills something on his pants
jokes.punchline: His sister immediately offers to help remove his pants, which is inappropriate sibling behavior
jokes.explanation: Continues the uncomfortable sibling dynamic with escalating inappropriateness. The pants removal suggestion crosses another boundary in front of Rachel.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! / That was unbelievable. / See? I told you. / Yeah. Sorry. / I don't believe they're brother and sister. / They're brother and sister?! / I guess you'll have to cancel your date. And call their mother.
jokes.setup: The group witnesses Danny and Krista's inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Escalating shock and Phoebe's suggestion to call their mother as if reporting children
jokes.explanation: Good payoff of the building tension with multiple character reactions. Phoebe's 'call their mother' suggestion treats adult behavior like children misbehaving.

jokes.text: What's this? A Canadian coin? Get out of here!
jokes.setup: Phoebe is inspecting donations in her charity bucket
jokes.punchline: She rejects a Canadian coin as if it's completely worthless foreign currency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's increasingly strict donation standards. Her outrage at foreign currency shows her becoming unreasonably picky.

jokes.text: You can leave the 'hurt bunny' look over there too.
jokes.setup: Someone gives Phoebe a sad expression after being rejected
jokes.punchline: Phoebe dismisses their emotional manipulation with the specific phrase 'hurt bunny look'
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific phrase showing Phoebe's street smarts recognizing emotional manipulation. 'Hurt bunny look' is original, evocative, and perfectly describes the expression.

jokes.text: That chick can't handle my corner.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is being moved to a less prominent charity collection spot
jokes.punchline: She's territorial about 'her corner' like a street dealer protecting turf
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Phoebe's street background with territorial language. Her possessiveness over a charity spot shows her competitive nature.

jokes.text: I'll give you one pointer. Look out for that bitch.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is being replaced by another charity collector
jokes.punchline: She warns her replacement about someone in harsh street language
jokes.explanation: Continues Phoebe's reversion to street behavior under pressure. The contrast between charity work and harsh warnings creates good character conflict.

jokes.text: You and your sister seem to have a very special bond— / Oh, great. That 'special bond' again.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to diplomatically address the inappropriate sibling behavior
jokes.punchline: Danny's frustrated response reveals this is an ongoing criticism he's tired of hearing
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that Danny is aware of and frustrated by this recurring criticism. His defensiveness suggests he knows it's inappropriate but doesn't want to change.

jokes.text: No, I have two sisters. But one has a very masculine energy.
jokes.setup: Danny asks if Rachel has brothers to explain sibling dynamics
jokes.punchline: Rachel describes her sister's 'masculine energy' as if that explains family dynamics
jokes.explanation: Rachel's attempt to relate to Danny's situation with pseudo-psychological language. The 'masculine energy' description is very 90s relationship speak.

jokes.text: Danny? Hurry up. The bath's getting cold.
jokes.setup: Danny is trying to convince Rachel their relationship is normal
jokes.punchline: His sister calls him to join her in a bath, the most inappropriate escalation possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for maximum cringe impact. The bath invitation comes exactly when Danny is trying to convince Rachel their relationship is normal, completely undermining his argument.

jokes.text: If they have a ball, you could stick razors in it... and teach them a new game: 'Gonna-Need-Stitches Ball.'
jokes.setup: Joey suggests giving projects to kids in the street to ruin their day
jokes.punchline: Chandler escalates to dangerous games involving razor blades with a darkly comic name
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation of their game invention tendency. 'Gonna-Need-Stitches Ball' is a memorably twisted name that captures their cavalier attitude toward safety.

jokes.text: Now we can go to the Ranger game last night!
jokes.setup: Joey excitedly announces he finished his work and they can go to the game
jokes.punchline: He says they can go to 'last night's' game, mixing up time references
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey confusion with time concepts. His excitement blinds him to the logical impossibility of attending last night's game now.

jokes.text: Also without somebody breathing down your neck all the livelong day!
jokes.setup: Ross suggests Joey focused better without distractions
jokes.punchline: Joey agrees but uses the old-fashioned phrase 'all the livelong day' dramatically
jokes.explanation: Joey's dramatic use of old-fashioned phrasing shows his tendency toward theatrical expression. The phrase choice is endearingly overwrought.

jokes.text: Could I be more sorry?
jokes.setup: Joey's script includes dialogue for a Chandler-like character
jokes.punchline: The line uses Chandler's signature 'Could I BE more...' speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke where Joey unconsciously writes Chandler's signature catchphrase into his script. Shows how the friends influence each other and Joey's lack of original writing ability.

jokes.text: I don't know. I am one sorry... polontologist.
jokes.setup: Joey's script continues with apologies between friends
jokes.punchline: He misspells/mispronounces 'paleontologist' as 'polontologist'
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey malapropism showing his poor grasp of Ross's profession despite years of friendship. The mispronunciation is believable and character-consistent.

jokes.text: A handsome man enters.
jokes.setup: Joey is reading his script about two friends reconciling
jokes.punchline: He introduces his own character as simply 'a handsome man'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey narcissism reducing his character description to his most valued trait. The simplicity and self-absorption is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And you. You co-created Fireball.
jokes.setup: Joey's character is thanking his friends in the script
jokes.punchline: He credits Chandler's character with 'co-creating Fireball' as a major accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey values the dangerous game as a legitimate creative achievement. His priorities are hilariously skewed toward entertainment over responsibility.

jokes.text: This took you all day? / No, this took five minutes. I spent the rest of the day coming up with new Ultimate Fireball!
jokes.setup: Ross is disappointed in Joey's minimal script progress
jokes.punchline: Joey admits he wrote it in five minutes and spent the day on game development instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect revelation of Joey's true priorities and time management. His honesty about spending the day on 'Ultimate Fireball' shows his complete misunderstanding of the assignment.

jokes.text: God, Tiffany, you smell so great. / It's my new perfume. Why don't you come closer where you can really appreciate it?
jokes.setup: Joey has written another script with two female characters
jokes.punchline: The dialogue quickly becomes sexually suggestive between the female characters
jokes.explanation: Standard male fantasy writing typical of Joey's character but not particularly clever. The dialogue is predictably clichéd adult film material.

jokes.text: Joey, you are sick. / This is disgusting. I'm not reading this. / Wait, wait, wait! The handsome man was about to enter!
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel react with disgust to Joey's inappropriate script
jokes.punchline: Joey protests that they're stopping right before his character's entrance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priorities - he's most upset about missing his own character's entrance rather than addressing the inappropriate content. Shows his narcissism and complete misunderstanding of their objections.

tmdb_overview: Rachel meets Danny's sister and is dismayed when the two seem a little too playful.
